text: The surgeon incises the peritoneum along the superior and inferior borders of the pancreas, mobilizing the pancreatic neck. The superior mesenteric vein and portal vein confluence are identified in the retropancreatic space.

The surgeon completes mobilization of the pancreas and transects the proximal pancreas using a stapler. The distal pancreas is then transected with an ultrasonic device, while the pancreatic duct is carefully transected using scissors. The splenic vessels are preserved during the procedure.

The surgeon drains the distal pancreas into the jejunum, constructing an end-to-side duct-to-mucosa pancreaticojejunostomy in layers. The posterior row of interrupted seromuscular sutures on the jejunum and the pancreatic capsule is made using 4-0 Prolene suture.
The surgeon creates a small anastomosis opposite the pancreatic duct on the wall of the jejunum, using interrupted sutures of 5-0 prolene to approximate the tissues.

A small-sized silicon catheter is placed through the anastomosis into the pancreatic duct and exits through a tunnel in the jejunal lobe. The surgeon completes the anastomosis by suturing the pancreatic capsule to the seromuscular layers of the jejunum using an anterior row of 4-0 prolene sutures.
